meaning: vt.使适应新环境,使服水土vi.服水土,适应
examples: Some earth plants that I will try to acclimate to the moon.
Once in France, a country he greatly admired, Christophe found it difficult to acclimate himself.
CUE, he said, will offer "natural" speech recognition that doesn't require the user to acclimate the system.
We have probably lost some of our ability to acclimate because of our technology.
Contextual help tips will also pop up to acclimate users to the phone and operating system.
